seoTitle: How to Remove the K or M Suffix From Numbers in Power BI
metaDescription: Learn how to disable Power BI's automatic K and M number rounding by switching Display units to None in the visual formatting pane.
intro: When your dashboard rounds a precise total down to a vague K or M abbreviation, executives lose the exact figures they need. This guide shows how to override Power BI's automatic display unit rounding on cards, charts, and tables so your numbers show in full. You'll also learn how to control decimal precision once the raw values are visible.

steps.heading: Select your visual
steps.body: Click directly on the Card, Line Chart, or Bar Chart visual whose numbers you want to adjust.

steps.heading: Find the display unit setting
steps.body: For cards, expand the Callout value section; for charts, expand Data labels and look for the Values formatting container.

steps.heading: Switch display units to None
steps.body: Open the Display units dropdown, which defaults to Auto, and select None instead.

steps.heading: Set decimal precision
steps.body: Scroll to the Value decimal places box and set it to 0 for a clean whole number, or adjust as needed for your reporting standard.

steps.heading: Verify the change
steps.body: Click surrounding slicers to confirm the full, un-truncated numbers still fit cleanly within your card and chart widths.

faq.question: Why does Power BI show K or M instead of full numbers by default?
faq.answer: The Display units setting defaults to Auto, which automatically abbreviates large numbers for compact readability unless manually overridden.

faq.question: Do I need to change this setting on every visual separately?
faq.answer: Yes, display unit settings are configured independently per visual, so you'll need to update each card or chart individually.

faq.question: Will removing the suffix affect my underlying data values?
faq.answer: No, this only changes the visual formatting and does not modify your actual data values or calculations.

faq.question: Can I still round to a specific number of decimals after removing K or M?
faq.answer: Yes, the Value decimal places setting lets you control precision independently once display units are set to None.
